content.answer:
Hi Ian and all,

Please find attached Dec SIP monthly doc for review.

For visibility we are working on 2 additional topics to be shared
separately by EOW next week for review:
1. Ensure local FBS SKUs can be exported via SIP (follow up topic from TP
monthly CB meeting): in progress of 1) refreshing impact potential with
regional WH team and local WH teams; 2) summarizing launch plan (system,
logistics, seller comms, local WH prep) and timeline
2. Direct selling: assess if it makes sense to build RTS channels
especially for large Direct lanes: in progress of finalizing RTS cost and
vol estimation

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question: Ensure local FBS SKUs can be exported via SIP (follow up topic from TP
Direct selling: assess if it makes sense to build RTS channels

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Slide 20

   -

   What is the latest on KR SIP? Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com> Ren Jiahui
   <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>


KRSIP is on track. System requirements have been signed off by KRCB/Reg SIP
team and reviewed with PM.

System readiness target Mar; business prep starts mid-Feb. No major
blocker/risk identified at this stage.



   -

   How do we intend to do grow NSS of CB sellers? Are we referring to CN
   export or Local market export? Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com> Wei
   Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>


We are referring to local market export (not CN export). This builds on our
structural cost advantage from SLS+SPX network scale; allowing us to offer
lower B2B and B2C rates than market alternatives.

We will start with ID/MY local SPX NSS customer base (~1.2k / 1.3k),
working with NSS team to identify their CB logistics needs and co-build
end-to-end solutions. Both ID/MY SPX teams are onboarded.

Additionally Zalora expressed interest in exploring NSS across its
operating lanes, particularly MY-PH; this is incremental to our core plan.
We are working with RBP team to identify more potential strategic customers
like Zalora.

[image: image.png]

Slide 36 Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>

   -

   TH seems very small. Should be much bigger especially on Fashion/Beauty
   front? In general think we have too little TH sellers on direct right now?


Agree. TH is currently under-scaled on Direct, especially in Fashion/Beauty.

This was due to seller acquisition capacity constraints, which previously
limited pitching to ~100 sellers before mid-Dec. The constraint is now
removed with mass pitching and upcoming RM pitching from Jan

   -

   Mass pitching: started in mid-Dec to ~260k eligible sellers. MTD 1,033
   sellers opened direct shops, 947 active selling (vs 69 active sellers in
   Nov). Next to continue the weekly pitching cadence.
   -

   1:1 pitching: planned in Jan-

Hi Ian,

Slide 20

   -

   What is the latest on KR SIP? Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com> Ren Jiahui
   <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>


KRSIP is on track. System requirements have been signed off by KRCB/Reg SIP
team and reviewed with PM.

System readiness target Mar; business prep starts mid-Feb. No major
blocker/risk identified at this stage.



   -

   How do we intend to do grow NSS of CB sellers? Are we referring to CN
   export or Local market export? Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.

Hi Ian,

Please find attached slides on the proposed launch plan for local FBS
export via SIP for your review.
After your approval, we will 1) include the proposal in the Jan CB monthly
update to Terence, 2) kick off launch preparations.

In summary:
Local FBS export potential estimated to be ~844k FBS SKUs, 89K ADO by
Dec’26, based on local FBS business growth projections in each market
We will start to launch MY/VN/PH as P1 from Mar-Jun;  to enable 18 FBS
export lanes by Dec’26
Key dependencies are 1) system readiness (upgrade SIP system and local WH
system); 2) legal/tax/finance clearance; we will focus on clearing these
two alongside other launch needs in Q1’26.

The data insights and launch plan have been synced with 1) regional WH
commercial/ops team; 2) export market local FBS business PICs.

Thank you. Best regards,

*Jill Huang Wan-Jung*

Shopee | Cross-Border | SLS

Mobile: +65 9813 3840

Address: 1 Fusionopolis Place #09-10, Galaxis, Singapore, 138522

content.question:
Hi Ian,



Slide 20

   - What is the latest on KR SIP?

Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com> Ren Jiahui
   <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>



KRSIP is on track. System requirements have been signed off by KRCB/Reg SIP
team and reviewed with PM.



System readiness target Mar; business prep starts mid-Feb. No major
blocker/risk identified at this stage.





I was referring to pitching status or at least getting a sense check of
seller willingness on this



Do not wish for a situation where we build a solution where the adoption is
low



   - How do we intend to do grow NSS of CB sellers?

Are we referring to CN
   export or Local market export?

Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com> Wei
   Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>



We are referring to local market export (not CN export). This builds on our
structural cost advantage from SLS+SPX network scale; allowing us to offer
lower B2B and B2C rates than market alternatives.



We will start with ID/MY local SPX NSS customer base (~1.2k / 1.3k),
working with NSS team to identify their CB logistics needs and co-build
end-to-end solutions. Both ID/MY SPX teams are onboarded.



Additionally Zalora expressed interest in exploring NSS across its
operating lanes, particularly MY-PH; this is incremental to our core plan.
We are working with RBP team to identify more potential strategic customers
like Zalora.





Slide 36 Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>

   - TH seems very small. Should be much bigger especially on
   Fashion/Beauty front?

In general think we have too little TH sellers on
   direct right now?

Agree. TH is currently under-scaled on Direct, especially in Fashion/Beauty.



This was due to seller acquisition capacity constraints, which previously
limited pitching to ~100 sellers before mid-Dec. The constraint is now
removed with mass pitching and upcoming RM pitching from Jan

o   Mass pitching: started in mid-Dec to ~260k eligible sellers. MTD 1,033
sellers opened direct shops, 947 active selling (vs 69 active sellers in
Nov). Next to continue the weekly pitching cadence.

o   1:1 pitching: planned in Jan-Mar, targeting ~800 priority sellers
chosen by import markets (Mall/brands across SG/MY/PH top 5 categories
including Fashion and Beauty). Target ~60% success rate owned by local RM
(part of local RM KPI).



With both mass and RM pitching active, we expect TH seller growth to
improve meaningfully over next 4-8 weeks, and catch up to peer export
markets by end Q1’26.

Slide 38 Huang Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com> Cuiwei Sun
<cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>

   - How can we grow seller acquisition even faster?

What are the blockers
   right now?

Current status: all eligible sellers have been pitched at least once via
mass or RM pitching. At this stage, the only way to materially accelerate
acquisition is to increase re-pitching success rate (currently ~5%; target
10-15%).



Blockers

o   Primary blocker: local RM incentive/capacity. Direct acquisition is not
part of local RM KPI (other than TH), successful pitching is not rewarded;

o   Secondary blockers: Seller incentives (e.g., low ads/rebate ROI vs
local shop), RM/CS capacity to support onboarding/operations; seller
education capacity - these limit scale-up but not on the critical path to
the faster acquisition.



Solutions

o   Short-term unblocks (Jan-Feb):

§  Reduce RM capacity constraint with 1) focus on Mall and brand sellers;
2) automate RM pitching workflow

§  Revamp seller incentives for Mall/managed sellers to close Direct ROI
gaps with local shops

o   Longer-term unblocks (By Q2-26): strengthen RM incentives 1) formalize
RM KPIs/incentives for Direct with support from export market country
managers and BD HODs; 2) work with HQ finance to enable export market to
earn a Direct service fee to expand RM and export team capacity.



Target to double Mall/managed seller pitching success rate across markets
by end-Mar, and reach ~200k active sellers before 9.9. (2x current, 10% of
local SIP sellers), 4 months ahead of original Dec’26 timeline target.



Slide 39 Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com> Huang Wan-Jung
<wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>

   - How about seller vouchers?

Seller vouchers are currently under-utilized on Direct. Voucher ADO
contribution is significantly lower than import market CNCB average. MYSG
is the only exception (summary table below).



*Metric*

*Lane*

*Direct Performance*

*Import Market CNCB*

*VS Direct*

*Voucher ADO*

* Contri%*

MYSG

40%

34%

▲6%

MYPH

13%

31%

▼18%

MYTH

25%

41%

▼15%

IDMY

5%

41%

▼36%

IDSG

6%

34%

▼28%

IDPH

5%

31%

▼26%

VNMY

7%

41%

▼34%

VNSG

9%

34%

▼25%

VNPH

24%

31%

▼7%



This is primarily a seller adoption and incentive design issue, not a
system blocker. MYSG high voucher adoption due to upsized co-share 50-50
voucher incentive scheme covering both BAU and spike days

o   BAU Co-share: a) 18% off, MS $10, Cap $20 b) New Sellers: 12%, MS $5,
Cap $5

o   Spike day Co-share a) 20% Discount Min Spend up to $50 Capped at $50 b)
50% Discount Min Spend $1 Capped at $3



Next steps: 1) work with local CB teams to replicate MYSG best practice to
other top lanes; 2) develop system solutions to default replication of
seller vouchers from local shop to Direct shop on an opt-out basis.



Right now we allow them to port over SKUs from local shop to export shop?
Should we also get them to port over their seller voucher?

Hi Ian:

Please see the reply in green below.

Slide 20

   - What is the latest on KR SIP? Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com> Ren Jiahui
   <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>



KRSIP is on track. System requirements have been signed off by KRCB/Reg SIP
team and reviewed with PM.



System readiness target Mar; business prep starts mid-Feb. No major
blocker/risk identified at this stage.





I was referring to pitching status or at least getting a sense check of
seller willingness on this



Do not wish for a situation where we build a solution where the adoption is
low


+Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com> @Junho Kim <junho.kim@shopee.com> @Changsong
You <changsong.you@shopee.com>


KRCB has surveyed 55 managed/brand sellers, 53% sellers show interest in
SIP.

For non-managed sellers, KRCB plans to replicate the opt-out model as CNSIP
and expect a higher adoption rate than managed sellers. (CNSIP adoption
rate >70%)


Regional SIP will work with KRCB for a pitching plan in Jan, and start
seller communication from mid-Feb to catch the system launch in Mar.



[Backup] Band seller survey results

Seller with no interests to SIP prefers a brand-owned operating model, with
SIP providing selective operational support only

   - Price setting controlled by brand HQ
   -

   Paid Ads requiring direct ROAS control and budget ownership
   -

   Shop listings managed centrally with identical listings across all
   markets (including designs)

For these sellers, SIP proposes to use the SIP system as a tool to migrate
shops for them at start, and return shops to sellers for self run if
sellers agree on this proposal.

Slide 39 Ailin Xie Huang Wan-Jung

   -

   How about seller vouchers?

Seller vouchers are currently under-utilized on Direct. Voucher ADO
contribution is significantly lower than import market CNCB average. MYSG
is the only exception (summary table below).



Metric

Lane

Direct Performance

content.question:
KRCB has surveyed 55 managed/brand sellers, 53% sellers show interest in
SIP.

For non-managed sellers, KRCB plans to replicate the opt-out model as CNSIP
and expect a higher adoption rate than managed sellers. (CNSIP adoption
rate >70%)



Regional SIP will work with KRCB for a pitching plan in Jan, and start
seller communication from mid-Feb to catch the system launch in Mar.





[Backup] Band seller survey results

Seller with no interests to SIP prefers a brand-owned operating model, with
SIP providing selective operational support only

   - Price setting controlled by brand HQ
   - Paid Ads requiring direct ROAS control and budget ownership
   - Shop listings managed centrally with identical listings across all
   markets (including designs)

For these sellers, SIP proposes to use the SIP system as a tool to migrate
shops for them at start, and return shops to sellers for self run if
sellers agree on this proposal.



This is hard to understand. How many SMT 1-3 sellers have we pitched to?
What is the contribution to platform, what is the success rate?

Agree to enhance system features to enable Direct sellers to replicate
local shop vouchers to Direct shops. We are working with CB BPM to finalize
a P1 product feature request by mid Jan.

We will also track the seller voucher adoption rate and work on a seller
communication/incentive plan with each market. We will prioritize the mall
and managed seller group who have a voucher gap between local market and
export market first.



When will have updates regarding this?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

This is hard to understand. How many SMT 1-3 sellers have we pitched to?
What is the contribution to platform, what is the success rate?

We have not kicked off the seller pitching yet. Above mentioned is a seller
sentiment survey to collect interests/concerns about the SIP model. The 55
sellers are all SMT2 and SMT3 brand sellers (18% KRCB ADO coverage,
excluding SMT2/3 resellers). Their opinions are representative of managed
sellers overall. We will bake their feedback into seller
pitching/acquisition materials to address their concerns early.

KRCB and SIP team are jointly working on a pitching plan based on the
seller segments, will be ready for review by 23 Jan (SIP Jan monthly
meeting). Actual pitching will start from mid-Feb to catch the system
launch in Mar.

Agree to enhance system features to enable Direct sellers to replicate
local shop vouchers to Direct shops. We are working with CB BPM to finalize
a P1 product feature request by mid Jan.

We will also track the seller voucher adoption rate and work on a seller
communication/incentive plan with each market. We will prioritize the mall
and managed seller group who have a voucher gap between local market and
export market first.



When will have updates regarding this?


We are finalizing feature design and impact estimation. CB BPM will email a
P0 request for your approval by this Friday (16 Jan), and proceed to RBX
approval in jan cycle. Target to launch the feature by Q1 with top
priority. Estimated dev effort is 1.5-2 months. CB BPM will closely monitor
the progress to mitigate any timeline risks.

*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Yu Zhang <yu.zhang@shopee.com>; Jianghong Liu <
jianghong.liu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Keljin

wei.qiang@shopee.com>; Chiong Zhi Ann <zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>; Huang
Wan-Jung <wanjung.huangwj@shopee.com>; Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>;
Joe Li <joe.liyc@shopee.com>; Lim Wan Ting <wanting.lim@shopee.com>; Ren
Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>; Eveline Seah <eveline.seahyl@shopee.com>;
Lee Jia Yi <jiayi.jylee@shopee.com>; Fandi Nie <fandi.nie@shopee.com>; Faye
Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>; Junho Kim <junho.kim@shopee.com>; Changsong You <
changsong.you@shopee.com>; Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>; Hyunji Seo <
hyunji.seo@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [Email update] Dec-25 SIP monthly meeting



Hi Ian,



*This is hard to understand. How many SMT 1-3 sellers have we pitched to?
What is the contribution to platform, what is the success rate?

*

We have not kicked off the seller pitching yet. Above mentioned is a seller
sentiment survey to collect interests/concerns about the SIP model. The 55
sellers are all SMT2 and SMT3 brand sellers (18% KRCB ADO coverage,
excluding SMT2/3 resellers). Their opinions are representative of managed
sellers overall. We will bake their feedback into seller
pitching/acquisition materials to address their concerns early.

KRCB and SIP team are jointly working on a pitching plan based on the
seller segments, will be ready for review by 23 Jan (SIP Jan monthly
meeting). Actual pitching will start from mid-Feb to catch the system
launch in Mar.



Agree to enhance system features to enable Direct sellers to replicate
local shop vouchers to Direct shops. We are working with CB BPM to finalize
a P1 product feature request by mid Jan.

We will also track the seller voucher adoption rate and work on a seller
communication/incentive plan with each market. We will prioritize the mall
and managed seller group who have a voucher gap between local market and
export market first.



*When will have updates regarding this?
